rationale: The cited paper is used as a direct methodological source, not just background. In the pilot study and Study 2, the authors explicitly state that their incidental regulatory focus questionnaire and net prevention-focus computation follow Sengupta and Zhou (2007). This operationalization is central to their process test that prevention focus mediates the effect of crowding on choice, so the citation materially supports design and inference.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Sengupta and Zhou's regulatory-focus measurement approach (including prevention vs. promotion item structure and net-score computation) to a new context of social crowding. This borrowed measure is used to test and support the proposed mediation mechanism.
